heroHeadline: Somalia: Opposition Declares President Loses Legitimacy After May 15 as Senior Officials Defect; EU NAVFOR Probes Piracy Incident off Xaafuun
heroSubtitle: With Somalia's presidential term now 23 days from expiry (May 15), the opposition formally declared on April 22 that President Hassan Sheikh Mohamud will lose all legitimacy after that date. The declaration followed mass defections of senior government officials — including the former NISA director and dismissed immigration chief — to the opposition at a meeting hosted by former President Sharif Sheikh Ahmed. Hawiye clan elders are now drafting a 'national salvation' plan for post-May 15 governance with no electoral framework agreed. In a simultaneous security development, EU NAVFOR Atalanta is investigating a possible armed boarding of a product tanker approximately 24 NM southeast of Xaafuun (Hafun) — the latest in a series of piracy incidents resurging since October 2025. The SNA/Macawisley counter-offensive to retake Adan Yabal (seized by Al-Shabaab on April 19) remains in mobilization phase as forces reposition from Beledweyne. AFRICOM's 56th Somalia airstrike of 2026 (on pace to exceed 200/year) targeted ISIS-Somalia in Puntland on April 17. Somalia faces its most dangerous convergence of governance failure, security deterioration, and humanitarian catastrophe since 2021 — with 6.5 million people facing acute hunger and only 13.4% of the $852M humanitarian response plan funded.
footerNote: Data sourced from ACLED, OCHA, UNSOM, AFRICOM, UN Monitoring Group, and major international outlets. Casualty figures contested where noted.
